mappings.rationaleAI: A broad introduction to computing, spanning hardware, programming, languages, and information processing systems (D, H).
Explores the theoretical foundations of computing including programming systems, language design, and artificial intelligence (D.2, D.3, D.5).
Traces the evolution from the abacus and mechanical calculators (B) through the development of modern electronic computers (D).
Directly addresses artificial intelligence including expert systems, natural-language processing, computer vision, and robotics (D.5).
Provides the theoretical framework for understanding communication and information that underpins all information processing systems (A, H).
Addresses mass data storage, database design, and search systems -- the infrastructure behind modern information retrieval (H.1.b, H.1.c, H.2.b).
Directly relevant to signal security, message authentication, and the history of code-making and code-breaking (I.9).
Examines electronic networks and telecommunications systems that form the backbone of modern communications (I.4, I.8).
Addresses photographic technology for both still and motion-picture imaging (G.1, G.2).
Explores the media landscape from traditional publishing (I.1) through modern broadcasting and digital communications.
Focuses on robotics as a key application of artificial intelligence, from industrial automation to autonomous systems (D.5).
Explores how technology has transformed music through recording and reproducing devices -- mechanical, magnetic, and optical (F).
Relevant to holography and the optical systems that underlie modern audio-visual technology (F.3, G.3).
The physics of waves underpins radio communications (I.5), radar (J.1), sonar (J.2), and modern telecommunications.
Relevant to sound recording technologies from phonographs to digital systems, and the principles of high-fidelity reproduction (F, F.5).
Addresses detection and remote sensing systems, including the electronic monitoring technologies that raise contemporary privacy concerns (J).
Traces the technological evolution of motion-picture photography and film production from early cinema to digital formats (G.2).
Spans printing technology from traditional plate-making and press work (E.3, E.4) to electronic and digital printing (H.1.d).
Addresses the legal frameworks -- copyright, patents, and digital rights -- that govern publishing (I.1) and information systems (H).
The physics of semiconductors and integrated circuits (K.1.a, K.1.c) provides the foundation for all modern computing hardware.
Examines dictionaries and lexicons as structured information systems for reference and education (H.2.c.i).
Nanotechnology enables continued miniaturization of integrated circuits and microprocessors at the heart of computing (K.1.c).
Addresses biometric sensing devices and pattern recognition, connecting physical measurement to information processing (K.2).
